summary: McKenzie reports on unexpected SEO success with MSN search, which has become his top sales source despite initially dismissing it. He notes a sudden spike in Google organic traffic suggesting a PageRank improvement, though conversion from Google searches is poor at 10% due to mismatched search intent. He also addresses users searching for registration keys, turning a potential piracy signal into a customer service opportunity.
key_points: MSN search proved surprisingly valuable for reaching non-technical audiences, becoming the #1 sales source despite being initially dismissed
Google organic traffic surged but converted poorly because visitors searching for educational content (Dolch sight word lists) had no intent to buy software
Search intent mismatch can make high traffic nearly worthless for conversions
People searching for registration keys may be confused customers rather than pirates, and addressing them publicly is smart customer service
money_quotes: Long time readers of this blog will probably remember me describing MSN as a very cheap date in terms of SEO. Well, perhaps I shouldn't have been so dismissive: she's now my #3 source of hits and my #1 source of sales for last week.
Apparently she's rather popular with non-technical audiences, just judging from the search queries I'm getting.
I'm flabbergasted that I get the rank I do for some of these: you would think "bingo cards" would be a fairly competitive search term but somehow I'm still on the first page.
Ironically, my conversion for Google organic searches is terrible (10%) because most people are looking for Dolch sight word lists and get them and leave without trying my software.
For the 5 people who found this blog looking for "Bingo Card Creator registration key": you'll find it in the mail you got after you purchased the software.
key_insight: A search engine dismissed as unimportant can become your best sales channel when its audience better matches your customer profile, while high-traffic sources with mismatched intent convert poorly.
